title: SOP: When to Escalate
content:
Escalation criteria:

1. Protected workflow failure: Any workflow in the PROTECTED_WORKFLOWS list must be escalated to L3 (Engineering/DevOps). Never auto-restart.

2. Recurrence threshold: If the same issue has recurred 3 or more times (configurable via RECURRENCE_ESCALATION_THRESHOLD), auto-escalate to L2 support for permanent root cause analysis.

3. Permission/auth errors: If tool calls fail with 401/403, escalate to L3 — likely a service account issue.

4. Data corruption suspected: If investigation reveals inconsistent data (e.g., row counts don't match, checksums fail), escalate to L2 with full RCA.

5. Multiple cascade failures: If 3+ downstream workflows fail due to the same root cause and auto-remediation doesn't resolve, escalate to L2.

Escalation actions:
- Create incident ticket via create_incident_ticket
- Send notification via send_notification to the appropriate team channel
- Set case owner_type to HUMAN_TEAM
- Stay hands-off — only add user updates to the ticket
